title: Pump drive system with hydraulic tappets
abstract: A cryogenic pump (10) comprising a shaft (26) disposed in a housing (18). The shaft rotates with respect to the housing, and the shaft includes an end (29) with an angled face (31). The pump includes a drive housing (20) at one end of the bearing housing. A tappet passage (33) is formed through the drive housing. A pushrod housing (22) connects to the drive housing. The pump includes a piston (35) and a tappet (37) sliding within the tappet passage. The tappet has a base end (43) disposed within the tappet passage and a rod end (41) extending below the tappet end of the drive housing. A fluid cavity (39) is in the tappet passage between the piston and the tappet. The pump includes a pushrod (34) connected to the tappet. The angled face of the shaft rotates and drives the piston toward the drive housing, pushing fluid within the fluid cavity against the tappet, driving the pushrod away from the drive housing.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A cryogenic pump (10) comprising: a bearing housing (18) having a shaft end (17) and a drive end (19); a shaft (26) having an upper end (27) and a lower end (29) disposed in the bearing housing at the shaft end and rotatable with respect to the bearing housing about a longitudinal axis (150), the lower end of the shaft including an angled face (31) oriented transverse to the longitudinal axis; a drive housing (20) having a piston end (21) and a tappet end (23), the drive housing disposed at the drive end of the bearing housing, at least one tappet passage (33) being formed through the drive housing substantially along the longitudinal axis between the piston end and the tappet end; a pushrod housing (22) connected to the tappet end of the drive housing; at least one piston (35) slidably disposed at least partially within the at least one tappet passage, a cavity end (42) of the piston disposed within the tappet passage; at least one tappet (37) slidably disposed at least partially within the at least one tappet passage, the at least one tappet having a base end (43) disposed within the tappet passage and a rod end (41) extending below the tappet end of the drive housing, wherein a fluid cavity (39) substantially filled with a fluid is formed in the tappet passage between the cavity end of the piston and the base end of the tappet; and at least one pushrod (34) disposed within the pushrod housing and connected to the rod end of the at least one tappet; wherein, during an extend stroke, the angled face of the shaft rotates and dri ves the at least one piston toward the tappet end of the dri ve housing so as to push the fluid within the fluid cavity against the base end of the at least one tappet thereby driving the at least one tappet to drive the at least one pushrod away from the drive housing into an extended position. 2. The cryogenic pump of claim 1 , further comprising a manifold (46) connected to the pushrod housing, and wherein at least one 

 pushrod spring (38) is disposed between the manifold and the at least one pushrod so as to bias the pushrod toward the drive housing. 3. The cryogenic pump of claim 2, wherein during a retract stroke, the at least one pushrod spring drives the at least one pushrod toward the drive housing so as to push the tappet toward the piston end of the drive housing, thereby pushing the fluid within the fluid cavity against the cavity end of the at least one piston to drive a slipper end (44) of the piston away from the drive housing and into a retracted position. 4. The cryogenic pump of any of the preceding claims, further comprising: a slipper (54) rotatably connected to a slipper end (44) of the piston opposite the cavity end and extending above the piston end of the drive housing, the at least one slipper disposed between the piston and the angled face of the shaft so as to slide along the angled face when the shaft rotates; wherein the at least one piston includes a piston fluid channel (62) running between the cavity end and the slipper end, and the at least one slipper includes a slipper fluid channel (66) in fluid communication with the piston fluid channel, the piston fluid channel and the slipper fluid channel providing fluid communication between the fluid cavity and a slipper interface (61) formed between the at least one slipper and the angled face of the shaft. 5. The cryogenic pump of claim 4, further comprising at least one piston spring (70) disposed in the tappet passage between the cavity end of the piston and the base end of the tappet so as to bias the piston toward the angled face of the shaft. 6. The cryogenic pump of claim 4 or 5, further compri sing: a retainer plate (80) having a guide orifice (82) formed through the retainer plate and at least one slipper orifice (84) formed through the retainer plate radially outward from the guide orifice, wherein the at least one slipper is disposed through the at least one slipper orifice; and 

 a retainer spring (86) having a housing end (88) and a guide end (89), the retainer spring disposed within a retainer bore (45) formed in the piston end of the drive housing partially through the drive housing and connected to the drive housing at the housing end, the guide end of the retainer spring extending out of the retainer bore and disposed within the guide orifice of the retainer plate: wherein the retainer spring biases the retainer plate toward the shaft so as to press the at least one slipper toward the angled face of the shaft, and wherein the retainer plate is configured to nutate about the guide end of the retainer spring as the shaft rotates with respect to the bearing housing. 7. The cryogenic pump of claim 6, wherein the retainer spring includes a retainer guide (92) on the guide end, and wherein the retainer plate nutates about the retainer guide. 8. The cryogenic pump of any of the preceding claims, further comprising at least one vent channel (76) formed through the drive housing between the at least one tappet passage and a drain annulus (77), the at least one vent channel providing fluid communication between the fluid cavity and the drain annulus when the tappet is in the extended position so as to provide an outlet for the fluid within the fluid cavity and restrict downward movement of the tappet. 9. The cryogenic pump of any of the preceding claims, further comprising a check valve (74) providing selective fluid communication between a fluid reservoir (45) and the fluid cavity, the check valve configured to allow fluid to flow into the fluid cavity when the pressure within the fluid cavity is lower than the pressure within the fluid reservoir. 10. The cryogenic pump of any of the preceding claims, wherein a diameter of the at least one piston is different than a diameter of the at least one tappet.
